questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.prompt: Which of the following best describes the governance and capital structure of the New Development Bank (NDB)?

options.text: Voting power is distributed based on the capital contribution of each member, similar to the IMF.
The five founding members hold permanent veto power over all infrastructure project approvals.
It ensures equal representation and voting power for all its founding members.
Only the five original BRICS nations are permitted to hold shares in the bank's authorized capital.

questions.explanation: The NDB was designed to be different from traditional Bretton Woods institutions by ensuring equal representation and voting power for its founding members (Brazil, Russia, India, China, and South Africa). While it has an authorized capital of $100 Billion, it is open to other UN members, but the founding principle emphasizes equal power among the original five.
questions.takeaway: The NDB's core democratic principle is equal voting power among its founders.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: New Development Bank (HQ: Shanghai, China): Fund infrastructure in BRICS and emerging economies, ensure equal representation and voting power for all members.

questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.prompt: In the context of the New Development Bank (NDB), which country was recently admitted as its 9th member?
questions.explanation: Algeria recently became the 9th member of the NDB. Uruguay is currently a prospective member (admitted by the Board but yet to deposit the instrument of accession). Bangladesh was an earlier addition.
questions.takeaway: Algeria is the newest (9th) member of the NDB, while Uruguay's status remains prospective.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: Algeria became 9th member of NDB. Uruguay is listed as the prospective member (admitted by NDB’s Board of Governors but will officially become a member after depositing its instrument of accession).
